data.title: TomTom Traffic Index finds U.S. among least congested countries. 15th edition released on Jan. 21, 2026.
data.description: In Amsterdam, Netherlands, on January 21, 2026, TomTom (TOM2) unveiled the 15th edition of the TomTom Traffic Index. This report, a comprehensive examination of global traffic patterns and commuting habits, marks another milestone for the company.

data.title: Deutsche Telekom Security partners with Akamai to enhance cybersecurity with microsegmentation and API security.
data.description: Akamai and Deutsche Telekom have joined forces to enhance their cybersecurity services by incorporating microsegmentation and API security solutions. This collaboration aims to provide advanced protection against cyber threats and ensure the security of organizations' sensitive data. The integration of microsegmentation and API security technologies will strengthen their defense mechanisms, enabling them to better safeguard against increasingly sophisticated attacks. This partnership signifies a commitment to delivering robust cybersecurity solutions in the face of rising digital threats. The incorporation of these technologies will bolster their security offerings and enhance their ability to fend off cyber risks effectively. This collaboration highlights the importance of innovative cybersecurity solutions in an ever-evolving threat landscape. Organizations can now benefit from this enhanced cybersecurity suite to mitigate risks and protect their digital assets effectively.

data.title: Wall Street analysts predict Halliburton will report Q4 earnings of 55 cents per share, down from 70 cents last year, with revenue estimated at $5.41 billion compared to $5.61 billion a year ago.
data.description: Halliburton Company (NYSE: HAL) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings before the start of trading on Wednesday, Jan. 21. Analysts are predicting that the Texas-based company will report earnings of 55 cents per share for the quarter, a decrease from 70 cents per share in the same period last year. The consensus among analysts is that Halliburton's quarterly revenue will total $5.41 billion, down from $5.61 billion in the previous year. Despite this, the company has surpassed revenue expectations in the last three quarters, but has only outperformed in four of the past 10 quarters overall. Following this news, shares of Halliburton saw a 1.6% decline, closing at $32.06 on Tuesday. For further insights, interested individuals can find more information on analyst ratings on the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Stocks to watch: Netflix, Johnson & Johnson. JNJ expected to report $2.46 EPS on $24.16B revenue. GameStop shares rise.
data.description: U.S. stock futures are showing a rise this morning, with investor attention likely to focus on certain stocks. Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) is expected to announce quarterly earnings of $2.46 per share with revenue reaching $24.16 billion before the market opens. The company's shares saw a slight decline of 0.3% to $217.50 in after-hours trading. In extended trading on Tuesday, GameStop Corp. (NYSE: GME) experienced an increase in shares following CEO Ryan Cohen's disclosure. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson to report Q4 earnings of $2.47 per share on Jan. 21, with revenue estimate at $24.16 billion.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on Wednesday, Jan. 21. Analysts anticipate earnings of $2.47 per share, up from $2.04 per share in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for the company's quarterly revenue is $24.16 billion compared to $22.52 billion last year. In recent news, on Jan. 14, Johnson & Johnson unveiled preliminary findings from the Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 study of Tecvayli for the treatment of multiple myeloma. The company's shares closed at $218.21 on Tuesday, experiencing a 0.2% drop. Readers can find more details on analyst ratings at Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock futures rose after Trump's Greenland threat, breaking key support for S&P 500 and Nasdaq. Nvidia and Tesla dropped.
data.description: Major stock indices including the S&P 500 and Nasdaq experienced a significant drop following President Trump's statements regarding Greenland, with expectations building on his speech at Davos. Tech giants like Nvidia and Tesla saw a decline alongside other megacap companies. Netflix also showed a late drop in its stock value. The Dow Jones Futures show a rise after the sell-off triggered by the President's Greenland threats, with Tesla and Nvidia being among the companies that experienced a decline in their stocks.

data.description: With growing divisions in the cryptocurrency industry, there is a debate on whether to embrace flawed regulations currently or face stricter rules in the future.

data.title: Dell introduces purpose-built education PCs and programs to support schools in transforming education with AI.
data.description: In London, there is a pivotal shift occurring in the field of education with the advent of cutting-edge technologies like Generative AI. Dell Technologies, a longstanding leader in education technology, is spearheading this transformation by providing schools with innovative tools and programs tailored for the AI era. This initiative aims to prepare students and educators for upcoming opportunities. Demonstrating their dedication, Dell has bolstered their education portfolio to support this important shift.

data.title: Halper Sadeh LLC urges PEN, FONR, GDOT shareholders to reach out over rights discussion. Contact them soon. NY, Jan 21.
data.description: Investors are urged to reach out to the company promptly as time may be running out to protect their interests. A law firm in New York, Halper Sadeh LLC, is currently looking into several companies for potential breaches of federal securities laws. The investigation is focusing on possible violations that could impact shareholders. If you own stock in these firms, it is crucial to take prompt action to safeguard your rights. Stay informed and reach out for support to ensure your interests are protected.
